text.title: go-standard-layout Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-standard-layout as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: All code in a single package at the repo root (flat structure).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No package boundaries means any file can call any function. Adding internal/ later requires touching every import.

failure-mode.description: A single "utils" or "common" package that grows into a God package imported by everyone.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: God packages create circular dependency risks and make the codebase harder to understand and test in isolation.

failure-mode.description: Importing a parent layer from a child (e.g. repository importing service).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reverse dependencies create circular import errors or force awkward interface gymnastics to break the cycle.
